items.text:
🔍 *ENRICH REVIEW*  \[✨ ENRICHED\]

*Name:* Skellig Michael
*Country:* ireland
*Region:* kingdom of munster
*County:* County Kerry
*Type:* abbey
*Period:* 6th century
*Coords:* 51.77111111, -10.54055556
*Wikipedia:* https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Skellig_Michael
*Maps:* https://www.google.com/maps/search/Skellig%20Michael/@51.7707,-10.5395,15z
*Main Image:* https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/d/d8/Skellig_Michael03%28js%29.jpg/330px-Skellig_Michael03%28js%29.jpg
*Gallery:* 0 images
*Image Source:* wikipedia
*Has Wikipedia:* yes
*Has Google Places:* unknown
*Enrich Notes:* add source - https://heritageireland.ie/places-to-visit/sceilg-mhichil/

*Description:*
Skellig Michael, also called Great Skellig, is a twin-pinnacled crag 11.6 kilometres (7.2 mi) west of the Iveragh Peninsula in County Kerry, Ireland. The island is named after the archangel Michael; while "Skellig" is derived from the Irish language word sceilig, meaning a splinter of stone. Its twin island, Little Skellig, is smaller and inaccessible. The two islands were formed c. 374–360 million years ago during a period of mountain formation, along with the local MacGillycuddy's Reeks mountain range. They were later separated from the mainland by rising water levels.

_Listing 1 of 10_

items.text:
🔍 *ENRICH REVIEW*  \[✨ ENRICHED\]

*Name:* Newgrange Cursus
*Country:* ireland
*Region:* irelands ancient east
*County:* County Meath
*Type:* fort
*Period:* c. 3100 BC
*Coords:* 53.695135, -6.473093
*Wikipedia:* https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Newgrange_cursus
*Maps:* https://www.google.com/maps/search/Newgrange%20Cursus/@53.694,-6.476,15z
*Main Image:* https://img.castlecore.uk/newgrange-cursus.jpg
*Gallery:* 4 images
*Image Source:* CDN
*Has Wikipedia:* no
*Has Google Places:* unknown
*Enrich Notes:* update google places link - https://maps.app.goo.gl/AaZtVSpKoMd9GgC99

*Description:*
The Newgrange cursus is a Neolithic monument used as a ceremonial procession route within the Brú na Bóinne complex. The ancient trackway is 100m long and 20m wide. It is located at Newgrange, in County Meath, Ireland.

_Listing 2 of 10_
